profile_a.description: Single parent, unemployed, 2 children, renting privately, age 28, no health conditions
profile_a.situation: Single parent, unemployed, 2 children, renting privately, aged 25-60, no health conditions or disability

benefits.name: Universal Credit
benefits.reason: You are a single parent who is unemployed and renting. Universal Credit is designed for exactly this situation and covers living costs plus a housing element.
benefits.gov_rule_reference: Universal Credit eligibility: single claimant aged 25 or over, unemployed, with children

benefits.name: Child Benefit
benefits.reason: You are responsible for 2 children. Child Benefit is paid for every child and is separate from Universal Credit.
benefits.gov_rule_reference: Child Benefit: 25.60 per week for eldest child, 16.95 per week for each additional child

benefits.name: Free School Meals
benefits.reason: As a Universal Credit recipient who is unemployed, your children qualify for free school meals.
benefits.gov_rule_reference: Free School Meals: eligible if receiving Universal Credit with net earnings under 7,400 per year

result.disclaimer: These are estimates only. Actual amounts depend on your rent, council area, and other circumstances. Always verify eligibility at GOV.UK or with an advisor.

profile_b.description: Couple, one employed part-time, no children, renting, age 30-40, no health conditions
profile_b.situation: Couple, one partner employed part-time, no children, renting privately, aged 25-60, no health conditions

benefits.name: Universal Credit
benefits.reason: As a couple where one partner works part-time, you may qualify for Universal Credit depending on your combined earnings. Universal Credit tops up low earnings.
benefits.gov_rule_reference: Universal Credit: couple aged 25 or over, 617.60 per month standard allowance, reduced by 55p per pound earned above work allowance

result.disclaimer: These are estimates only. Universal Credit for couples depends heavily on combined earnings. Use the GOV.UK benefits calculator for a more accurate figure.

profile_c.description: Single person, unable to work due to health condition, renting, age 35, disability
profile_c.situation: Single person, unable to work due to long-term health condition or disability, renting privately, aged 25-60

benefits.name: Universal Credit
benefits.reason: You are single, unable to work, and renting. You may qualify for both the standard allowance and the limited capability for work-related activity (LCWRA) element.
benefits.gov_rule_reference: Universal Credit: standard allowance 393.45 plus LCWRA element 416.19 per month

benefits.name: Personal Independence Payment (PIP)
benefits.reason: If your health condition has lasted or is expected to last over 9 months and affects your daily living or mobility, you may qualify for PIP. This is assessed independently of income.
benefits.gov_rule_reference: PIP daily living standard rate 73.90 per week. Requires assessment of how condition affects daily activities.

result.disclaimer: PIP is shown as MEDIUM confidence because it requires a formal health assessment. Universal Credit LCWRA element also requires a Work Capability Assessment. Always verify at GOV.UK.
